steps.title: Find the key signal
steps.content: The passage opens with Dixson’s 2009 study on how ocean acidification affects Amphiprion percula fish behavior, then raises concerns about small sample sizes inflating the effect. Every sentence centers on acidification and fish behavior — the main topic must capture both.

steps.title: Test each choice
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘Loss of marine diversity due to overfishing.’: overfishing is never mentioned; the passage is about acidification, not species loss from fishing pressure. ✗
  • Choice B — ‘Changes in technologies used to capture fish.’: fishing technology is not discussed anywhere in the passage. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘Feeding patterns of top marine predators.’: Amphiprion percula (clownfish) is not a top predator, and the passage never discusses feeding patterns. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘Effects of ocean acidification on fish behavior.’: this is precisely what both cited studies investigate — Dixson’s 2009 study of behavioral effects and Clark’s 2022 review of methodological concerns about that same topic. ✓
That matches choice D — D is correct.
